en.extract:
Turati's boubou (Laniarius turatii) is a species of bird in the family Malaconotidae.
It is found in Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, and Sierra Leone.
Its natural habitat is moist savanna.
The common name and Latin binomial commemorates the Milanese banker Ercole Turati  (1829-1881) who also collected natural history specimens.

'''Turati's boubou''' ('''''Laniarius turatii''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Malaconotidae]].
It is found in [[Guinea]], [[Guinea-Bissau]], and [[Sierra Leone]].
Its natural [[habitat]] is moist [[savanna]].

The common name and Latin binomial commemorates the Milanese banker [[Ercole Turati]]  (1829-1881) who also collected natural history specimens.<ref>{{cite book|last=Beolens|first=Bo|title=Whose Bird? Men and Women Commemorated in the Common Names of Birds|year=2003|publisher=Christopher Helm|location=London|page=344|author2=Watkins, Michael }}</ref>
